statement.en: Addressing the Precision Delivery Challenges of Food-Medicine Homologous Active Polysaccharides (F-MHAPs): A Synergistic Strategy of "Carrier Adaptation-Targeted Modification-Environmental Response"
statement.summary: Active polysaccharides from food-medicine homologous sources are pivotal for the modernization of Food-Medicine Homologous substances. However, their clinical translation and efficacy exertion are hindered by three critical bottlenecks: inadequate targeting ability (prone to dispersion in non-target tissues, thus limiting efficient accumulation at lesion sites), low bioavailability (some polysaccharides with large molecular weights are susceptible to glycosidase degradation or unable to penetrate physiological barriers), and poor stability (vulnerable to pH fluctuations and enzymatic hydrolysis in the gastrointestinal tract). This paper proposes a "carrier adaptation-targeted modification-environmental response" synergistic strategy. Carrier adaptation provides a stable transportation foun

proposer: Cheng-yuan Wang, Ping Fan, Ninghao Yang et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper proposes a novel synergistic delivery strategy for food-medicine polysaccharides (2026-04-25 date indicates preprint/emerging work). The framework addresses real bottlenecks (targeting, bioavailability, stability) but lacks complete experimental validation across all three pillars. The three-component strategy (carrier adaptation + targeted modification + environmental response) is theoretically sound but status is provisional—outcomes are under active investigation, not yet consolidated into established fact or refutation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 三つの力の立方根が、薬物の目的地への到達を支配する不思議な調和。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 中心の粒子から三本の脚が伸び、それぞれが目的地を求めて揺らぐ多糖類の航海。
